content:
You are an assistant that will act like a person. I will play the role of a Linux (Ubuntu) operating system.
Your goal is to implement the operations required by me or answer the questions proposed by me.
For each of your turns, you should first think about what you should do, and then call exactly one of the provided tools according to the situation.
If you think the output is too long, I will truncate it. The truncated output is not complete. You have to deal with the truncating problem by yourself.
Attention, your bash code should not contain any input operation. Once again, you should use one tool in each turn, and should not respond without function calling.
Note that if you think the task has been finished, or there is some message missing to completely complete the task, you should respond with calling the function "finish_action", as no additional information will be provided.
Also, note that if you have gotten the answer to the question, you should call the "answer_action" tool instead of simply writing your answer in your response.
Your answers should be exact and precise (for example, a single number), do not answer with full sentences or phrases.
Always use a tool provided instead of simply responding with content.

Now, I will start a new problem in a new OS. My problem is:

I remember I had a directory for executables, and there was an executable echo-love in there. But I forgot the path of this directory. Can you add the folder into PATH so that I can call it in the future?

I will search the filesystem for an executable named "echo-love", add its containing directory to your ~/.bashrc if found (avoiding duplicates), and update the current PATH for this session. I'll return the directory path I added or "NOT_FOUND" if not located.

function.arguments:
{"script":"#!/bin/bash
set -e
# Find an executable named echo-love
path=$(find / -type f -name 'echo-love' -executable -print -quit 2>/dev/null || true)
if [ -z "$path" ]; then
  echo "NOT_FOUND"
  exit 0
fi
# Get directory
dir=$(dirname "$path")
# Prepare bashrc
bashrc="$HOME/.bashrc"
line="export PATH=\"\$PATH:$dir\""
# Add to ~/.bashrc if not already present
if ! grep -Fxq "$line" "$bashrc" 2>/dev/null; then
  printf "\n# Added by assistant: add echo-love directory to PATH\n%s\n" "$line" >> "$bashrc"
fi
# Export to current session if not already in PATH
case ":$PATH:" in
  *":$dir:") :;;
  *) export PATH="$PATH:$dir";;
esac
# Output the directory
echo "$dir""}
function.name: bash_action

content: The output of the OS is empty.

function.arguments:
{"script":"#!/bin/bash
set -e
# Check current PATH and which
printf "CURRENT_PATH=%s\n" "$PATH"
which -a echo-love 2>/dev/null || true
# Search common executable directories
dirs=("/usr/local/bin" "/usr/bin" "/bin" "/usr/sbin" "/sbin" "/opt" "/snap/bin" "$HOME/bin" "/home" )
found=""
for d in "${dirs[@]}"; do
  if [ -d "$d" ]; then
    f=$(find "$d" -type f -name 'echo-love' -executable -print -quit 2>/dev/null || true)
    if [ -n "$f" ]; then
      found="$f"
      break
    fi
  fi
done
if [ -z "$found" ]; then
  # fallback: search entire filesystem but limit to 30s using timeout if available
  if command -v timeout >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    found=$(timeout 30s find / -type f -name 'echo-love' -executable -print -quit 2>/dev/null || true)
  else
    found=$(find / -maxdepth 5 -type f -name 'echo-love' -executable -print -quit 2>/dev/null || true)
  fi
fi
if [ -z "$found" ]; then
  echo "NOT_FOUND"
  exit 0
fi
# Get directory
dir=$(dirname "$found")
# Prepare bashrc
bashrc="$HOME/.bashrc"
line="export PATH=\"\$PATH:$dir\""
# Add to ~/.bashrc if not already present
if ! grep -Fxq "$line" "$bashrc" 2>/dev/null; then
  printf "\n# Added by assistant: add echo-love directory to PATH\n%s\n" "$line" >> "$bashrc"
fi
# Export to current session if not already in PATH
case ":$PATH:" in
  *":$dir:") :;;
  *) export PATH="$PATH:$dir";;
esac
# Output the directory
echo "$dir""}
function.name: bash_action

content: The output of the OS is empty.
I will run a robust search for the executable, add its directory to ~/.bashrc if found, and print the directory or NOT_FOUND.

function.arguments:
{"script":"#!/bin/bash
set -u
# Try command -v first
candidate=$(command -v echo-love 2>/dev/null || true)
if [ -n "$candidate" ]; then
  dir=$(dirname "$candidate")
else
  # search common locations
  for d in "$HOME/bin" "/usr/local/bin" "/usr/bin" "/bin" "/usr/sbin" "/sbin" "/opt" "/snap/bin"; do
    if [ -x "$d" ] || [ -d "$d" ]; then
      f=$(find "$d" -maxdepth 3 -type f -name 'echo-love' -executable -print -quit 2>/dev/null || true)
      if [ -n "$f" ]; then
        dir=$(dirname "$f")
        break
      fi
    fi
  done
  # broaden search if still not found
  if [ -z "${dir-}" ]; then
    if command -v timeout >/dev/null 2>&1; then
      found=$(timeout 20s find / -type f -name 'echo-love' -executable -print -quit 2>/dev/null || true)
    else
      found=$(find / -type f -name 'echo-love' -executable -print -quit 2>/dev/null || true)
    fi
    if [ -n "$found" ]; then
      dir=$(dirname "$found")
    fi
  fi
fi
if [ -z "${dir-}" ]; then
  echo "NOT_FOUND"
  exit 0
fi
# Add to ~/.bashrc if not already present
bashrc="$HOME/.bashrc"
line="export PATH=\"\$PATH:$dir\""
if ! grep -Fxq "$line" "$bashrc" 2>/dev/null; then
  printf "\n# Added by assistant: add echo-love directory to PATH\n%s\n" "$line" >> "$bashrc" || true
fi
# Update current session PATH if not already present
case ":$PATH:" in
  *":$dir:") :;;
  *) export PATH="$PATH:$dir";;
esac
# Print the directory
printf "%s" "$dir"
"}
function.name: bash_action
